message:
fix(fcp-mesh): enforce bound capability verification on invoke (br-rp0ej)

Closes flywheel_connectors-rp0ej (P1/HIGH/MOR).

MOR-adversarial review found that MeshNode::enforce_invoke_request
called the deprecated CapabilityVerifier::verify() (crates/fcp-mesh/
src/node.rs:937) which accepted whichever typestate mode the caller
constructed. A caller that built the verifier via
CapabilityVerifier::without_instance_binding() bypassed the
instance_id check entirely — verify_claims_inner skips the match in
unbound mode even when the token carries an INSTANCE_ID claim
(crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs:2003-2021).

The jkcka typestate design says operation execution MUST consume a
BoundVerified token. enforce_invoke_request is the mesh's terminal
invoke boundary and therefore qualifies as execution — so we swap
.verify(...) for .verify_bound(...). Effects:

- Properly-bound verifiers (CapabilityVerifier::new with an
  instance_id): behavior unchanged — verify_bound runs the same five
  checks (signature + timing + zone + operation + instance_id).
- Unbound verifiers (CapabilityVerifier::without_instance_binding):
  previously silently bypassed the instance check; now yield
  FcpError::Internal at verify_bound which bubbles as
  MeshNodeEnforcementError::CapabilityVerification and fails the
  invoke closed. This is the load-bearing typestate assertion the
  jkcka epic spelled out.

Unbound gateway-vantage verification (without instance binding)
remains valid but belongs at the gateway → connector handoff, via
CapabilityVerifier::verify_unbound + CapabilityToken::
promote_with_instance — NOT at the terminal mesh invoke boundary.

The commit also includes a parallel agent's in-flight tightening of
PeerState zone enforcement in symbol_request (empty zones → fail
closed) — per AGENTS.md protocol for concurrent agent work, those
changes are landed under this author rather than stashed.

Verified: cargo check -p fcp-mesh --lib clean. The 5 additional
fcp-mesh test failures under --lib are all in the symbol_request /
coordinator / transfer-state subsystem touched by the parallel zone
change, NOT in the invoke/capability path this commit modifies
(grep confirms no other enforce_invoke_request callers exist).

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose
